title: Arizona Basic Search and Rescue
description: This is the 5th edition (dated October 9, 2024) of the 200-page PDF document, written and copyrighted by members of the Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association, Ltd. It is an introduction to the basics of searching for missing persons in wilderness environments. It is aimed at members of
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Inland Search Management for AZ SAR Coordinators
description: 18th edition (9/14/2024) by ASARCA.  Designed to be used by  search managers in conjunction with Win CASIE III and ICS-SAR.   Forms the basis of a 5 day 40-hour Inland Search Mgmt course.  Participants are expected to study the manual before class.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Arizona Basic Search and Rescue Handbook
description: This is is the 4th edition (9/7/2023) written and copyrighted by members of the Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association, Ltd.   Students are expected to study the manual before class.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Inland Search Management (non-Arizona specific version)
description: This is the non-Arizona specific version of "Inland Search Management for AZ SAR Coordinators" described in the previous entry. Updated October 21, 2021.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Initial Response Incident Commander Wilderness Search
description: 2nd Edition 2021 written and copyrighted by ASARCA.   A guide to managing searches for missing persons in wilderness environments during the Initial Response phase of a search.  It is designed to be used by search managers in conjunction with Win-CASIE III.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Find 'Em: A Guide for Planning the Missing Person Incident Response
description: This is a 300-page PDF document, written by Paul Anderson, Aaron Dick, David Lovelock, and Greg Stiles. It is devoted to managing searches for missing persons, primarily in wilderness environments. It is designed to be used by prospective and experienced Planning Section Chiefs (PSCs) and their Unit
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Find 'Em Presentation
description: The Power Point slides of the presentation given by Greg Stiles at the 2014 Syrotuck Symposium held in Woodcliff Lake, NJ.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Moving INLAND SAR into the 21st Century
description: The Power Point slides of the presentation given by Dan O'Connor of NEWSAR at the 2014 Syrotuck Symposium held in Woodcliff Lake, NJ.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Search Management Action Plan
description: Search Management Action Plan is a two-page document (PDF) developed at the Grand Canyon National Park. Updated 2013. It is an excellent timeline tool designed to cover the Initial, Concurrent, and Successive actions of a search. Reprinted with permission Bil Vandergraff & Ken Phillips - All Rights
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: 2010 ICS Forms Comments
description: This PDF document, written by David Lovelock, is an attempt to summarize the major differences between the old ICS forms and the new ones released at the end of 2010. Updated 5/20/2011.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Helicopter Experiments: Mountain
description: This is a copy of the 1991 document ``MOUNTAIN SEARCHES: EFFECTIVENESS OF HELICOPTERS'' that describes the research that was conducted in Pima County, AZ, to measure the effectiveness of United States Air Force Air Rescue crews searching for lost persons in a rugged mountain environment of Southern
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Helicopter Experiments: Desert
description: This is a copy of the 1981 document "DESERT SEARCHES: EFFECTIVENESS OF HELICOPTERS" that describes the research that was conducted in Pima County, AZ, to establish a conservative measurement of the probability of detection, POD, of the United States Air Force helicopter rescue teams searching Sonora
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Southern Arizona Search and Rescue History: 1901–2000
description:
This is a 600 page PDF document writtn by Butch Farabee, with the assisatnce of David Lovelock. It
                                chronicles over 1000 events, some large and involved, such as when two Army Air Servicemen flying between San Diego and Tucson went missing over the remote desert on Dec
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: The U.K. Missing Person Behaviour Study, March 2011
description: This is a 66-page document (PDF) by Dave Perkins, Pete Roberts, and Ged Feeney. A total of 1271 search incidents in the UK are analyzed, and some subject behaviors differ considerably from US and Canadian counterparts.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Inland Search Management (Non AZ)
description: This is the non-Arizona specific version of “Inland Search Management for AZ SAR Coordinators”.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Find ‘Em
description: Find ‘Em: A Guide for Planning the Missing Person Incident Response.  This guide is written to help the PSC on large, complex search incidents perform critical functions and manage their section in the most professional, effective and efficient manner possible. It is copyrighted by the ASARCA, Ltd.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Searching For Subjects with Autism
description: The National Autism Association has compiled a SAR checklist when the missing subject is autistic.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Managing Spontaneous Volunteers in SAR
description: Suggestions on how to manage spontaneous volunteers — volunteers who are not currently affiliated with a recognized SAR organizations — that arrive unannounced at a search incident.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: SAR Taskbooks
description: SAR Taskbooks  ASARCA has rolled out some of the new Arizona SAR Taskbooks.  Completion of a Taskbook allows the SAR Volunteer to gain their Arizona Certification for that taskbook.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Helicopter Rescue Techniques
description: This 112-page PDF document, written by Ken Phillips, Branch Chief of Search and Rescue, National Park Service, is a training manual prepared by the United States Department of the Interior, National Park Service for the National SAR Academy (NSARA). It deals with Civilian Public Safety and Military
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Helicopter Risk Continuum
description: These three PDF documents, written by Tim Kovacs, Maricopa County Sheriff's Office Mountain Rescue/Central Arizona Mountain Rescue, cover the decision making process for employing helicopters in search and rescue.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Incident Response Checklist
description: Incident Response Checklist is a two-page document (PDF) written by Paul Anderson, Denali National Park and Preserve, Alaska. It is a checklist of items to be performed by the IC from the moment the subject is reported missing. This document is part of Win CASIE III.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Interviewing a Subject Found Alive
description: If the subject is found alive then a post-incident interview with the found subject should be conducted to determine what happened. This document suggests ways of doing this. This document is part of Win CASIE III.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title:
Investigative Task
                                    List
description:
Investigative Task List is a two-page document (PDF) compiled by experienced search managers. It is a checklist of items to be considered when investigating the disappearance of a subject. This document is part of Win CASIE
                                    III.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: ISRID statistics and UK missing person incidents
description: This review, written by Dave Perkins, looks at the ISRID statistics from the point of view of the UK search manager, and wonders what they provide that is above and beyond the statistics produced within the UK.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Managing Spontaneous Volunteers in Search and Rescue
description: This 28-page PDF document, written by Paul Anderson, Aaron Dick, Eric Johnson, David Lovelock, Richard Martin, and Jeremy Ramirez, suggests how to manage spontaneous volunteers—volunteers who are not currently affiliated with a recognized SAR organization—that arrive unannounced at a search incident
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Map Datums For SAR Planning and Operations
description: These three PDF documents are written by Art Pundt, Coconino County Sheriff's Office, Search and Rescue Unit, Flagstaff, Arizona. The first document discusses Finding Map or GPS Datums; Common Datums; and Maps, Datums, and SAR Resources. The second is a one-page briefing sheet. The third summarizes
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Objectives, Strategies, and Tactics in Search and Rescue
description: This PDF file, written by David Lovelock, attempts to clarify the meaning and use of the terms Objectives, Strategies, and Tactics, with examples from Search and Rescue.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Questions to ask a Lost Subject who self-reports by Cell Phone
description: This is a checklist (PDF) written by Eric E. Johnson, Pima County Sheriff's Department, Tucson, AZ. With the increased usage of cell phones, more and more subjects are reporting themselves lost via a cell phone. Interviewing these subject requires a different mind set from interviewing a typical rep
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title:
POC
                                    ≠ POA
description: POC ≠ POA is an article (PDF) written by David Lovelock, University of Arizona, Tucson, AZ. It explains why POC (Probability of Containment) and POA (Probability of Area) are not the same.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: SARTopo Beginners How To
description: SARTopo Beginners How To is an article (PDF) compiled by David Lovelock. It is a cheatsheet for beginners just starting SARTopo.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: Some Thoughts on Multiple-Subject Searches
description: Some Thoughts on Multiple-Subject Searches is a two-page document (PDF) that summarizes a discussion between Paul Anderson, Aaron Dick, David Lovelock, Dan O'Connor, Dave Perkins, Pete Roberts, and Rick Toman, on the basics of how to analyze and respond to a multiple-person search.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: The Pseudoscience Of Land Search Probability Theories
description: A document written by Hugh Dougher. This paper contends that many of the land search probability theories and debates reflect pseudoscience, and asserts that strict adherence to the maritime search probability model isn't appropriate for land search.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association

title: WinCASIEIII Operator Job Action Sheet
description: A two-page document summarizing the qualifications and actions that should be taken by a WinCASIEIII operator during a Route and Location Search, a consensus, and an Area Search.
authors: Arizona Search and Rescue Coordinators Association
